deterministic_setup.reason:
com.sun.jersey:jersey-core:1.14 declares org.osgi.core 4.2.0 as a provided dependency, its manifest imports org.osgi.framework version [1.5,2), and com.sun.jersey.core.osgi.OsgiRegistry loads org.osgi.framework.BundleReference during Activator.start(); the existing org.osgi:osgi_R4_core:1.0 test dep
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8875 [Automation] java run fails for com.sun.jersey:jersey-core:1.14; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: If the old org.osgi:osgi_R4_core:1.0 dependency remains on the test classpath, there will be duplicate org.osgi.framework classes. Local Gradle verification should confirm classpath ordering does not still select an incompatible OSGi API for any generated OSGi tests.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Jersey Core 1.14 moved its provided OSGi API from org.osgi:osgi_R4_core:1.0 to org.osgi:org.osgi.core:4.2.0, and its OSGi activator path requires org.osgi.framework.BundleReference, which the old R4 core artifact does not contain.
